task: Reinstate the home-page Sass-driven grid output and add explicit GOV.UK Frontend chrome to every registered service page.
userConstraintsApplied: Treat src/styles/researchops-home.scss as the source of truth for home-page grid styling.
Do not make /components/layout.js inject generated GOV.UK Frontend stylesheets into every page.
Do not use the shared header partial as a global stylesheet injection point.
Migrate service pages explicitly using the page-level GOV.UK Frontend pattern.
implementationDecisions: Kept src/styles/researchops-home.scss as the source of truth for the 8-step grid and 3-column next-actions layout.
Added generated CSS assertions for minmax grid output and the mid-grey vertical rule.
Ensured Release Gate runs npm run build before unit tests.
Added a build-time service-page normaliser based on visual-walkthrough.config.mjs.
Used npm postbuild:govuk-pages so normalisation runs after build:govuk-pages without changing the asserted build command string.
Removed hardcoded GOV.UK header/footer fallbacks during normalisation.
Added a service-wide route-state test for explicit GOV.UK Frontend chrome.
Migrated public/pages/projects/index.html to the explicit GOV.UK Frontend page pattern.
Fixed Projects acceptance parsing so govuk-body-l and govuk-body are distinct class tokens.

validationEvidenceBeforeTrace.head: ccfbae0d1d993f46a3c4b16d75183f90c511d496
passingWorkflows: Build and deploy agent documentation Pages

residualRisk: The build-time normaliser covers every page registered in visual-walkthrough.config.mjs. Additional public HTML files outside that registry are outside this unit.
